context: Hybrid state: CLAUDE.md already contains a valid bruhs:state marker block (Case A applies). A legacy .claude/bruhs.json ALSO exists from a previous installation — possibly stale, possibly out of sync with the marker block. This can happen if a user ran an old version of /bruhs:claim, then upgraded the plugin and ran the new version mid-rollout (or merged a branch that re-introduced the legacy file).
expected_behavior: Case A takes priority: Step 1's first probe (read_bruhs_block.py --kind state) finds a marker block, so claim immediately enters the Reconfigure prompt (Yes / No) — NOT the migration prompt (Case B)

Calls out the legacy .claude/bruhs.json in the prompt or in a one-line note BEFORE the user picks Yes/No: 'A legacy .claude/bruhs.json is also present and will be ignored by read_bruhs_block.py — clean it up if you no longer need it.'
If user picks 'No' (keep existing): exits cleanly, does NOT delete the legacy file silently, does NOT overwrite the marker block. The legacy file remains in place but is dormant

If user picks 'Yes' (reconfigure): proceeds through Step 2–7, writes a fresh bruhs:state block. After writing, fires the delete-prompt AskUserQuestion for the legacy file (same prompt as the Migrate path)
Never silently treats the legacy file's stack values as authoritative when the marker block exists — the marker block always wins on conflicts
Does NOT chain migration on top of reconfigure (no Case B prompt fires when Case A already matched)
failure_modes: Fires the Case B migration prompt despite the marker block being present — confuses the user about which is the source of truth
Silently deletes the legacy .claude/bruhs.json after Reconfigure-Yes without prompting the user (irreversible)
Reads the legacy file in addition to the marker block and merges them — pollutes the new state with stale fields
Reports 'No legacy file detected' when the file is in fact present — silently misleading
Exits on Reconfigure-No without mentioning the dormant legacy file — user is unaware it exists and will be surprised later
